rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 12, 2019 line 1. PETITIONER CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment GRANTED. Moving party to prepare order and judgment. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: POODLES, INC., VS. ROGER C. KUHN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant All Animals Properties, Lp, 1333 Ninth Avenue Llc, All Animals Emergency Hospital, Inc., Notice Of Motion To Vacate Courts Order Of April 22, 2016
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 12, 2019 line 8. DEFENDANT ROGER KUHN, MICHAEL INA, EUGENE LYNCH, SHERMAN WONG, LAWRENCE BENNETT, ALL ANIMALS PROPERTIES, LP, 1333 NINTH AVENUE, LLC, ALL ANIMALS EMERGENCY HOSPITAL, INC., JOSEPH LYNCH Defendant All Animals Properties, Lp, 1333 Ninth Avenue Llc, All Animals Emergency Hospital, Inc., Notice Of Motion To Vacate Courts Order Of April 22, 2016 GRANTED as follows: Only the corporate Defendant shall be allowed to use up to $250,000 to defendant this action (up to $125,000 for work performed between July 2019 and September 2019l and $125,000 for work performed between October 2019 and trial) upon presentation of the invoices. $250,000 shall be deposited in a separate account. Parties to agree on a single person to be responsible to receiving the invoices and disbursing the payments within 10 days of the receipt of the approval from the corporate defendant as to the amount of the fees billed. Approval shall be in writing specifying the agreed to amount as to each invoice. Further, Plaintiff's opposition is stricken. The Court gave Plaintiff an opportunity to file an amended brief in compliance with CRC 2.100 et seq. Plaintiff defied the Court's order and submitted another non-complaint brief, e.g. CRC 2.108, while lines are numbered 1-27, each page contains 32 lines. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: OLUSOJI FANOIKI VS. 1081-1087 MISSISSIPPI STREET ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Why Sanctions Should Not Be Imposed
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 12, 2019 line 3. Order To Show Cause Why Sanctions Should Not Be Imposed Preliminary Injunction is Lifted (request to lift the injunction has previously been denied without prejudice and is renewed by the Defendants). Events and multiple hearings surrounding the proper functioning of the HOA and the impact it has on its members show that the balance of harms in this case (one of the two elements required for a preliminary injunction) dramatically favors the lifting of the injunction. Order to Show Cause is hereby discharged and the preliminary injunction is lifted. The HOA may proceed with all of its operations, including those previously enjoined. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA DE VRIES VS. ERIC LORD ZERO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 12, 2019 line 5. DEFENDANT ERIC ZERO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show that it is entitled to a judgment as a matter of law. Specifically, Defendants cite no authority and present no fact that an alternative, i.e. cure was required under the circumstances of this case. The Court also notes that Defendant does not suggest what the potential cure option would be possible under the agreement. = (501/CFH)
